revision.text: #redirect[[en:Ceramic_technology]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]
焼き物などを作る工業。 セメントなども含まれる。

焼き物などを作る工業。 セメントなども含まれる。
窯業系サイディング材という分類有り。 ・工期の速さや優れた意匠性などの特長 ・大工・左官職人の不足や施工品質の安定性を求める市場環境 ・戸建て住宅の外壁シェアの約７割を占めるまでに市場が拡大

'''窯業'''（ようぎょう）は、[[陶磁器]]、[[ガラス]]、[[セメント]]など、非金属原料を高熱処理して作るものを製造する[[工業]]。窯（かま）を使用するため、'''窯業'''と呼ばれる。
近年では、'''窯業系サイディング材'''と呼ばれる[[建築]]用材料が普及しつつあり、工期の速さや優れた[[意匠]]性などの特長、[[大工]]・[[左官]]職人の不足や施工品質の安定性を求める市場環境から、戸建て住宅の外壁シェアの約７割を占めるまでに市場が拡大している。

'''窯業'''（ようぎょう）は、[[陶磁器]]、[[ガラス]]、[[セメント]]など、非金属原料を高熱処理して作るものを製造する[[工業]]。[[窯]]（かま）を使用するため、'''窯業'''と呼ばれる。
近年では、'''窯業系サイディング材'''と呼ばれる[[建築]]用材料が普及しつつあり、工期の速さや優れた[[意匠]]性などの特長、[[大工]]・[[左官]]職人の不足や施工品質の安定性を求める市場環境から、戸建て住宅の外壁シェアの約７割を占めるまでに市場が拡大している。
[[Category:産業|ようきょう]]
{{tech-stub}}

'''窯業'''（ようぎょう）は、[[粘土]]、[[ケイ砂]]、[[石灰岩]]などから[[陶磁器]]、[[瓦]]、[[ガラス]]、[[セメント]]など、非金属原料を高熱処理して作るものを製造する[[工業]]。[[窯]]（かま）を使用するため、'''窯業'''と呼ばれる。窯の燃料は、伝統的には[[薪]][[炭]]、[[石炭]]であったが、近年では[[石油]]を使用するボイラーや[[電気]]窯が普及している。
[[Image:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|中国[[雲南省]]の窯業。[[粘土瓦]]を生産する。乾燥させた後、窯で焼きあげた円筒状の焼き物を二分して瓦にする。]]
古代では草葺、茅葺であった家屋の屋根がほとんどであったが、中世からは貴族、武士、僧侶の一部で、[[粘土瓦]]を使用した屋根が普及し始めた。[[磁器]]や[[クリスタルガラス]]の生産が可能になると、調度品として高く評価され、美術品としての価値を生むようにもなった。[[陶工]]、[[陶芸家]]という職業も社会的地位を確立した。ただし、現在の[[開発途上国]]の小規模な伝統的窯業にあっては、[[陶器]]などの調度品、食器とならんで、建築のための[[瓦]]の生産量が多い。
近年では、'''窯業系サイディング材'''と呼ばれる[[建築]]用材料が普及しつつあり、工期の速さや優れた[[意匠]]性などの特長、[[大工]]・[[左官]]職人の不足や施工品質の安定性を求める市場環境から、戸建て住宅の外壁シェアの約７割を占めるまでに市場が拡大している。
==関連項目== *[[セラミックス]] *[[ガラス]] *[[瓦]] *窯業[[高等学校]]：現在は[[愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校]]のみ。 *窯業[[大学校]]：[[佐賀県立有田窯業大学校]]のみがある。
[[Category:窯業|*ようきょう]] [[Category:製造業|ようきょう]]
{{tech-stub}}

近年では、'''窯業系サイディング材'''と呼ばれる[[建築]]用材料が普及しつつあり、工期の速さや優れた[[意匠]]性などの特長、[[大工]]・[[左官]]職人の不足や施工品質の安定性を求める市場環境から、戸建て住宅の外壁シェアの約７割を占めるまでに市場が拡大している。

revision.comment: /* 関連項目 */ 佐賀県立有田窯業大学校は「専修学校」です
revision.text:
'''窯業'''（ようぎょう）は、[[粘土]]、[[ケイ砂]]、[[石灰岩]]などから[[陶磁器]]、[[瓦]]、[[ガラス]]、[[セメント]]など、非金属原料を高熱処理して作るものを製造する[[工業]]。[[窯]]（かま）を使用するため、'''窯業'''と呼ばれる。窯の燃料は、伝統的には[[薪]][[炭]]、[[石炭]]であったが、近年では[[石油]]を使用するボイラーや[[電気]]窯が普及している。
[[Image:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|中国[[雲南省]]の窯業。[[粘土瓦]]を生産する。乾燥させた後、窯で焼きあげた円筒状の焼き物を二分して瓦にする。]]
古代では草葺、茅葺であった家屋の屋根がほとんどであったが、中世からは貴族、武士、僧侶の一部で、[[粘土瓦]]を使用した屋根が普及し始めた。[[磁器]]や[[クリスタルガラス]]の生産が可能になると、調度品として高く評価され、美術品としての価値を生むようにもなった。[[陶工]]、[[陶芸家]]という職業も社会的地位を確立した。ただし、現在の[[開発途上国]]の小規模な伝統的窯業にあっては、[[陶器]]などの調度品、食器とならんで、建築のための[[瓦]]の生産量が多い。
近年では、'''[[窯業系サイディング材]]'''と呼ばれる[[建築材料]]が普及しつつあり、工期の速さや優れた[[意匠]]性などの特長、[[大工]]・[[左官]]職人の不足や施工品質の安定性を求める市場環境から、戸建て住宅の外壁シェアの約７割を占めるまでに市場が拡大している。
==関連項目== *[[セラミックス]] *[[ガラス]] *[[瓦]] *[[砥石]] *窯業[[高等学校]]：現在は[[愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校]]のみ。 *窯業[[専修学校]]：[[佐賀県立有田窯業大学校]]のみがある。
[[Category:窯業|*ようきょう]] [[Category:製造業|ようきょう]]
{{tech-stub}}

[[ファイル:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|中国[[雲南省]]の窯業。[[粘土瓦]]を生産する。乾燥させた後、窯で焼きあげた円筒状の焼き物を二分して瓦にする。]] [[ファイル:Aichi Tokoname24n4272.jpg|230x230px|thumb|伝統的燃料を使用する窯 （[[愛知県]][[常滑市]]）]]
'''窯業'''（ようぎょう）は、[[粘土]]、[[ケイ砂]]、[[石灰岩]]などから[[陶磁器]]、[[瓦]]、[[ガラス]]、[[セメント]]など、非金属原料を高熱処理して作るものを製造する[[工業]]。[[窯]]（かま）を使用するため、'''窯業'''と呼ばれる。窯の燃料は、伝統的には[[薪]][[炭]]、[[石炭]]であったが、近年では[[石油]]を使用するボイラーや[[電気]]窯が普及している。
古代では草葺、茅葺であった家屋の屋根がほとんどであったが、中世からは貴族、武士、僧侶の一部で、[[粘土瓦]]を使用した屋根が普及し始めた。[[磁器]]や[[クリスタルガラス]]の生産が可能になると、調度品として高く評価され、美術品としての価値を生むようにもなった。[[陶工]]、[[陶芸家]]という職業も社会的地位を確立した。ただし、現在の[[開発途上国]]の小規模な伝統的窯業にあっては、[[陶器]]などの調度品、食器とならんで、建築のための[[瓦]]の生産量が多い。
近年では、'''[[窯業系サイディング材]]'''と呼ばれる[[建築材料]]が普及しつつあり、工期の速さや優れた[[意匠]]性などの特長、[[大工]]・[[左官]]職人の不足や施工品質の安定性を求める市場環境から、戸建て住宅の外壁シェアの約７割を占めるまでに市場が拡大している。
==関連項目== *[[セラミックス]] *[[ガラス]] *[[瓦]] *[[砥石]] *窯業[[高等学校]]：現在は[[愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校]]のみ。 *窯業[[専修学校]]：[[佐賀県立有田窯業大学校]]のみがある。
[[Category:窯業|*ようきょう]] [[Category:製造業|ようきょう]]
{{tech-stub}}

古代では[[草葺]]、[[茅葺]]であった家屋の[[屋根]]がほとんどであったが、中世からは貴族、武士、僧侶の一部で、[[粘土瓦]]を使用した屋根が普及し始めた。[[磁器]]や[[クリスタルガラス]]の生産が可能になると、調度品として高く評価され、美術品としての価値を生むようにもなった。[[陶工]]、[[陶芸家]]という職業も社会的地位を確立した。美術品ないし工芸品としては、 [[七宝焼き|七宝]]や[[ガラス]]も同様である。ただし、現在の[[開発途上国]]の小規模な伝統的窯業にあっては、[[陶器]]などの調度品、食器とならんで、建築のための[[瓦]]の生産量が多い。
近年では、'''[[窯業系サイディング材]]'''と呼ばれる[[建築材料]]が普及しつつあり、工期の速さや優れた[[意匠]]性などの特長、[[大工]]・[[左官]]職人の不足や施工品質の安定性を求める市場環境から、戸建て住宅の外壁シェアの約７割を占めるまでに市場が拡大している。
== 関連項目 == * [[陶磁器]] * [[七宝焼き|七宝]] * [[ガラス]] * [[セラミックス]] * [[瓦]] * [[砥石]] * [[セラミック顔料]] * [[窯業高等学校]]：現在は[[愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校]]のみ。 * 窯業[[専修学校]]：[[佐賀県立有田窯業大学校]]のみがある。
{{Tech-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:ようきよう}} [[Category:窯業|*ようきよう]] [[Category:製造業]]

[[ファイル:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|中国[[雲南省]]の窯業。[[粘土瓦]]を生産する。乾燥させた後、窯で焼きあげた円筒状の焼き物を二分して瓦にする。]] '''窯業'''（ようぎょう）は、[[粘土]]、[[ケイ砂]]、[[石灰岩]]などの[[非金属]]原料を高熱処理して、[[陶磁器]]、[[瓦]]、[[ガラス]]、[[セメント]]などの[[セラミックス]]（セラミック、窯業製品とも呼ぶ。）を製造する[[工業]]<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 「世界大百科事典 第２版、大辞林 第三版」]</ref>。[[窯]]（かま）を使用するため、'''窯業'''と呼ばれる。窯の燃料は、伝統的には[[薪]][[炭]]、[[石炭]]であったが、近年では[[石油]]を使用するボイラーや[[電気]]窯が普及している。

revision.text:
{{Expand English|date=August 2024}} [[File:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|Traditional kiln work in {{ill|Yunnan Province|ja|雲南省}}, China. Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]
'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of {{ill|clay|ja|粘土}}, {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂}}, {{ill|limestone|ja|石灰岩}}, and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属}} to produce {{ill|ceramics|ja|陶磁器}}, {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, {{ill|glass|ja|ガラス}}, {{ill|cement|ja|セメント}}, and other {{ill|ceramics|ja|セラミックス}} (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of {{ill|kilns|ja|窯}}. Traditional fuels for kilns included {{ill|firewood|ja|薪}}, {{ill|charcoal|ja|炭}}, and {{ill|coal|ja|石炭}}, but modern kilns often use {{ill|oil-fired boilers|ja|石油}} or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺}} or {{ill|straw roofs|ja|茅葺}}, but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce {{ill|porcelain|ja|磁器}} and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, {{ill|cloisonné|ja|七宝}} and {{ill|glass art|ja|ガラス}} were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many {{ill|developing countries|ja|開発途上国}} with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of {{ill|carpenters|ja|大工}} and {{ill|plasterers|ja|左官}} have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校}} remains the sole institution of its kind.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ceramics|ja|陶磁器}} * {{ill|Cloisonné|ja|七宝}} * {{ill|Glass|ja|ガラス}} * {{ill|Ceramics (material)|ja|セラミックス}} * {{ill|Tiles|ja|瓦}}, {{ill|Bricks|ja|煉瓦}} * {{ill|Whetstone|ja|砥石}} * {{ill|Ceramic pigments|ja|セラミック顔料}}

{{Expand English|date=August 2024}} [[File:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|Traditional kiln work in {{ill|Yunnan Province|ja|雲南省}}, China. Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]
'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of {{ill|clay|ja|粘土}}, {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂}}, {{ill|limestone|ja|石灰岩}}, and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属}} to produce {{ill|ceramics|ja|陶磁器}}, {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, {{ill|glass|ja|ガラス}}, {{ill|cement|ja|セメント}}, and other {{ill|ceramics|ja|セラミックス}} (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of {{ill|kilns|ja|窯}}. Traditional fuels for kilns included {{ill|firewood|ja|薪}}, {{ill|charcoal|ja|炭}}, and {{ill|coal|ja|石炭}}, but modern kilns often use {{ill|oil-fired boilers|ja|石油}} or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺}} or {{ill|straw roofs|ja|茅葺}}, but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce {{ill|porcelain|ja|磁器}} and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, {{ill|cloisonné|ja|七宝}} and {{ill|glass art|ja|ガラス}} were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many {{ill|developing countries|ja|開発途上国}} with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of {{ill|carpenters|ja|大工}} and {{ill|plasterers|ja|左官}} have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校}} remains the sole institution of its kind.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{Expand English|date=August 2024}} [[File:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|Traditional kiln work in {{ill|Yunnan Province|ja|雲南省}}, China. Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]
'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of {{ill|clay|ja|粘土}}, {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂}}, {{ill|limestone|ja|石灰岩}}, and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属}} to produce {{ill|ceramics|ja|陶磁器}}, {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, {{ill|glass|ja|ガラス}}, {{ill|cement|ja|セメント}}, and other {{ill|ceramics|ja|セラミックス}} (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of {{ill|kilns|ja|窯}}. Traditional fuels for kilns included {{ill|firewood|ja|薪}}, {{ill|charcoal|ja|炭}}, and {{ill|coal|ja|石炭}}, but modern kilns often use {{ill|oil-fired boilers|ja|石油}} or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺}} or {{ill|straw roofs|ja|茅葺}}, but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce {{ill|porcelain|ja|磁器}} and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, {{ill|cloisonné|ja|七宝}} and {{ill|glass art|ja|ガラス}} were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many {{ill|developing countries|ja|開発途上国}} with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of {{ill|carpenters|ja|大工}} and {{ill|plasterers|ja|左官}} have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, {{ill|cloisonné|ja|七宝}} and {{ill|glass art|ja|ガラス}} were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many {{ill|developing countries|ja|開発途上国}} with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of {{ill|carpenters|ja|大工}} and {{ill|plasterers|ja|左官}} have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.

* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of {{ill|clay|ja|粘土}}, {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂}}, {{ill|limestone|ja|石灰岩}}, and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属}} to produce {{ill|ceramics|ja|陶磁器}}, {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, {{ill|glass|ja|ガラス}}, {{ill|cement|ja|セメント}}, and other {{ill|ceramics|ja|セラミックス}} (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of {{ill|kilns|ja|窯}}. Traditional fuels for kilns included {{ill|firewood|ja|薪}}, {{ill|charcoal|ja|炭}}, and {{ill|coal|ja|石炭}}, but modern kilns often use {{ill|oil-fired boilers|ja|石油}} or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺}} or {{ill|straw roofs|ja|茅葺}}, but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce {{ill|porcelain|ja|磁器}} and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, {{ill|cloisonné|ja|七宝}} and {{ill|glass art|ja|ガラス}} were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many {{ill|developing countries|ja|開発途上国}} with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of {{ill|carpenters|ja|大工}} and {{ill|plasterers|ja|左官}} have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

{{Expand English|date=August 2024}} [[File:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|Traditional kiln work in {{ill|Yunnan Province|ja|雲南省}}, China. Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]
'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of {{ill|clay|ja|粘土}}, {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂}}, {{ill|limestone|ja|石灰岩}}, and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属}} to produce {{ill|ceramics|ja|陶磁器}}, {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, {{ill|glass|ja|ガラス}}, {{ill|cement|ja|セメント}}, and other {{ill|ceramics|ja|セラミックス}} (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of {{ill|kilns|ja|窯}}. Traditional fuels for kilns included {{ill|firewood|ja|薪}}, {{ill|charcoal|ja|炭}}, and {{ill|coal|ja|石炭}}, but modern kilns often use {{ill|oil-fired boilers|ja|石油}} or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺}} or {{ill|straw roofs|ja|茅葺}}, but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce {{ill|porcelain|ja|磁器}} and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, {{ill|cloisonné|ja|七宝}} and {{ill|glass art|ja|ガラス}} were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many {{ill|developing countries|ja|開発途上国}} with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of {{ill|carpenters|ja|大工}} and {{ill|plasterers|ja|左官}} have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校}} remains the sole institution of its kind.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Ceramics|ja|陶磁器}} * {{ill|Cloisonné|ja|七宝}} * {{ill|Glass|ja|ガラス}} * {{ill|Ceramics (material)|ja|セラミックス}} * {{ill|Tiles|ja|瓦}}, {{ill|Bricks|ja|煉瓦}} * {{ill|Whetstone|ja|砥石}} * {{ill|Ceramic pigments|ja|セラミック顔料}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank}}

* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校}} remains the sole institution of its kind.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ceramics|ja|陶磁器}} * {{ill|Cloisonné|ja|七宝}} * {{ill|Glass|ja|ガラス}} * {{ill|Ceramics (material)|ja|セラミックス}} * {{ill|Tiles|ja|瓦}}, {{ill|Bricks|ja|煉瓦}} * {{ill|Whetstone|ja|砥石}} * {{ill|Ceramic pigments|ja|セラミック顔料}}

{{Expand English|date=August 2024}} [[File:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|Traditional kiln work in {{ill|Yunnan Province|ja|雲南省}}, China. Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]
'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of {{ill|clay|ja|粘土}}, {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂}}, {{ill|limestone|ja|石灰岩}}, and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属}} to produce {{ill|ceramics|ja|陶磁器}}, {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, {{ill|glass|ja|ガラス}}, {{ill|cement|ja|セメント}}, and other {{ill|ceramics|ja|セラミックス}} (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of {{ill|kilns|ja|窯}}. Traditional fuels for kilns included {{ill|firewood|ja|薪}}, {{ill|charcoal|ja|炭}}, and {{ill|coal|ja|石炭}}, but modern kilns often use {{ill|oil-fired boilers|ja|石油}} or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺}} or {{ill|straw roofs|ja|茅葺}}, but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce {{ill|porcelain|ja|磁器}} and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, {{ill|cloisonné|ja|七宝}} and {{ill|glass art|ja|ガラス}} were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many {{ill|developing countries|ja|開発途上国}} with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like {{ill|tiles|ja|瓦}}, alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of {{ill|carpenters|ja|大工}} and {{ill|plasterers|ja|左官}} have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|qq=|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]
'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of [[:en:Clay|clay]], {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quartz sand|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石英|1=quartz|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43010}}, [[:en:Limestone|limestone]], and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/non-metallic materials|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 非金属元素|1=nonmetal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19600}} to produce [[:en:Pottery|ceramics]], [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], [[:en:Glass|glass]], [[:en:Cement|cement]], and other [[:en:Ceramic|ceramics]] (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of [[:en:Kiln|kilns]]. Traditional fuels for kilns included [[:en:Firewood|firewood]], [[:en:Char (chemistry)|charcoal]], and [[:en:Coal|coal]], but modern kilns often use [[:en:Petroleum|oil-fired boilers]] or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/electric kilns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯|1=kiln|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1724341}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/thatch|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茅葺|1=thatched roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q874531}} or [[:en:Thatching|straw roofs]], but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|qq=|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce [[:en:Porcelain|porcelain]] and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal glass|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クリスタル・ガラス|1=lead glass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392551}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/potters|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陶芸家|1=ceramicist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7541856}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic artists|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ceramicist|WD=Q7541856}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, [[:en:Cloisonné|cloisonné]] and [[:en:Glass|glass art]] were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many [[:en:Developing country|developing countries]] with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siding materials|qq=|1=siding materials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597728}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of [[:en:Carpentry|carpenters]] and [[:en:Plasterwork|plasterers]] have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|qq=|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|qq=|1=Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11384748}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

The term originates from the use of [[:en:Kiln|kilns]]. Traditional fuels for kilns included [[:en:Firewood|firewood]], [[:en:Char (chemistry)|charcoal]], and [[:en:Coal|coal]], but modern kilns often use [[:en:Petroleum|oil-fired boilers]] or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/electric kilns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯|1=kiln|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1724341}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/thatch|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茅葺|1=thatched roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q874531}} or [[:en:Thatching|straw roofs]], but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|qq=|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce [[:en:Porcelain|porcelain]] and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal glass|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クリスタル・ガラス|1=lead glass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392551}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/potters|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陶芸家|1=ceramicist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7541856}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic artists|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ceramicist|WD=Q7541856}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, [[:en:Cloisonné|cloisonné]] and [[:en:Glass|glass art]] were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many [[:en:Developing country|developing countries]] with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siding materials|qq=|1=siding materials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597728}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of [[:en:Carpentry|carpenters]] and [[:en:Plasterwork|plasterers]] have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|qq=|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|qq=|1=Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11384748}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窯業]][[zh:窯業]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28578238]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28578238]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand English|date=August 2024}} [[File:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|Traditional kiln work in [[:en:Yunnan|Yunnan Province]], China. Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]
'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of [[:en:Clay|clay]], {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quartz sand|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石英|1=quartz|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43010}}, [[:en:Limestone|limestone]], and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/non-metallic materials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 非金属元素|1=nonmetal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19600}} to produce [[:en:Pottery|ceramics]], [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], [[:en:Glass|glass]], [[:en:Cement|cement]], and other [[:en:Ceramic|ceramics]] (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of [[:en:Kiln|kilns]]. Traditional fuels for kilns included [[:en:Firewood|firewood]], [[:en:Char (chemistry)|charcoal]], and [[:en:Coal|coal]], but modern kilns often use [[:en:Petroleum|oil-fired boilers]] or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/electric kilns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯|1=kiln|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1724341}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/thatch|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茅葺|1=thatched roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q874531}} or [[:en:Thatching|straw roofs]], but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce [[:en:Porcelain|porcelain]] and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal glass|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クリスタル・ガラス|1=lead glass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392551}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/potters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陶芸家|1=ceramicist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7541856}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic artists|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ceramicist|WD=Q7541856}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, [[:en:Cloisonné|cloisonné]] and [[:en:Glass|glass art]] were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many [[:en:Developing country|developing countries]] with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siding materials|1=siding materials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597728}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of [[:en:Carpentry|carpenters]] and [[:en:Plasterwork|plasterers]] have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|1=Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11384748}} remains the sole institution of its kind.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[:en:Pottery|Ceramics]] * [[:en:Cloisonné|Cloisonné]] * [[:en:Glass|Glass]] * [[:en:Ceramic|Ceramics (material)]] * [[:en:Roof tiles|Tiles]], [[:en:Brick|Bricks]] * [[:en:Sharpening stone|Whetstone]] * {{ill|Ceramic pigments|ja|セラミック顔料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic pigments|1=Ceramic pigments|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11314503}}

* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|1=Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11384748}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窯業]][[zh:窯業]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28578238]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28578238]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand English|date=August 2024}} [[File:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|Traditional kiln work in [[:en:Yunnan|Yunnan Province]], China. Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]
'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of [[:en:Clay|clay]], {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quartz sand|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石英|1=quartz|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43010}}, [[:en:Limestone|limestone]], and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/non-metallic materials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 非金属元素|1=nonmetal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19600}} to produce [[:en:Pottery|ceramics]], [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], [[:en:Glass|glass]], [[:en:Cement|cement]], and other [[:en:Ceramic|ceramics]] (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of [[:en:Kiln|kilns]]. Traditional fuels for kilns included [[:en:Firewood|firewood]], [[:en:Char (chemistry)|charcoal]], and [[:en:Coal|coal]], but modern kilns often use [[:en:Petroleum|oil-fired boilers]] or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/electric kilns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯|1=kiln|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1724341}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/thatch|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茅葺|1=thatched roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q874531}} or [[:en:Thatching|straw roofs]], but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce [[:en:Porcelain|porcelain]] and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal glass|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クリスタル・ガラス|1=lead glass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392551}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/potters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陶芸家|1=ceramicist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7541856}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic artists|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ceramicist|WD=Q7541856}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, [[:en:Cloisonné|cloisonné]] and [[:en:Glass|glass art]] were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many [[:en:Developing country|developing countries]] with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siding materials|1=siding materials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597728}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of [[:en:Carpentry|carpenters]] and [[:en:Plasterwork|plasterers]] have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|1=Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11384748}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

The term originates from the use of [[:en:Kiln|kilns]]. Traditional fuels for kilns included [[:en:Firewood|firewood]], [[:en:Char (chemistry)|charcoal]], and [[:en:Coal|coal]], but modern kilns often use [[:en:Petroleum|oil-fired boilers]] or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/electric kilns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯|1=kiln|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1724341}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/thatch|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茅葺|1=thatched roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q874531}} or [[:en:Thatching|straw roofs]], but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce [[:en:Porcelain|porcelain]] and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal glass|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クリスタル・ガラス|1=lead glass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392551}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/potters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陶芸家|1=ceramicist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7541856}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic artists|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ceramicist|WD=Q7541856}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, [[:en:Cloisonné|cloisonné]] and [[:en:Glass|glass art]] were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many [[:en:Developing country|developing countries]] with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siding materials|1=siding materials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597728}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of [[:en:Carpentry|carpenters]] and [[:en:Plasterwork|plasterers]] have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|1=Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11384748}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

<!--Kilnwork--><!--Kilnwork--><!--Kilnwork--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:窯業]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:窯業]] {{wikidata link|Q28578238}} [[Category:Ceramics industry]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Manufacturing]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窯業]][[zh:窯業]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窯業]][[zh:窯業]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窯業]][[zh:窯業]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28578238]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28578238]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand English|date=August 2024}} [[File:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|Traditional kiln work in [[:en:Yunnan|Yunnan Province]], China. Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]
'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of [[:en:Clay|clay]], {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quartz sand|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石英|1=quartz|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43010}}, [[:en:Limestone|limestone]], and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/non-metallic materials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 非金属元素|1=nonmetal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19600}} to produce [[:en:Pottery|ceramics]], [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], [[:en:Glass|glass]], [[:en:Cement|cement]], and other [[:en:Ceramic|ceramics]] (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of [[:en:Kiln|kilns]]. Traditional fuels for kilns included [[:en:Firewood|firewood]], [[:en:Char (chemistry)|charcoal]], and [[:en:Coal|coal]], but modern kilns often use [[:en:Petroleum|oil-fired boilers]] or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/electric kilns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯|1=kiln|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1724341}}.
In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/thatch|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茅葺|1=thatched roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q874531}} or [[:en:Thatching|straw roofs]], but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce [[:en:Porcelain|porcelain]] and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal glass|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クリスタル・ガラス|1=lead glass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392551}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/potters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陶芸家|1=ceramicist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7541856}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic artists|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ceramicist|WD=Q7541856}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, [[:en:Cloisonné|cloisonné]] and [[:en:Glass|glass art]] were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many [[:en:Developing country|developing countries]] with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like [[:en:Roof tiles|tiles]], alongside ceramics and tableware.
In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siding materials|1=siding materials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597728}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of [[:en:Carpentry|carpenters]] and [[:en:Plasterwork|plasterers]] have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.
== Education ==
* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct. * '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|1=Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11384748}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

The term originates from the use of {{ill|Kiln|en|Kiln|WD=Q1724341|lt=kilns}}. Traditional fuels for kilns included {{ill|Firewood|en|Firewood|WD=Q35808|lt=firewood}}, {{ill|Char (chemistry)|en|Char (chemistry)|WD=Q11567121|lt=charcoal}}, and {{ill|Coal|en|Coal|WD=Q24489|lt=coal}}, but modern kilns often use {{ill|Petroleum|en|Petroleum|WD=Q22656|lt=oil-fired boilers}} or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/electric kilns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯|1=kiln|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1724341}}.

In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/thatch|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茅葺|1=thatched roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q874531}} or {{ill|Thatching|en|Thatching|WD=Q874531|lt=straw roofs}}, but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce {{ill|Porcelain|en|Porcelain|WD=Q130693|lt=porcelain}} and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal glass|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クリスタル・ガラス|1=lead glass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392551}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. [[File:20040906yunankama.jpg|230px|thumb|Traditional kiln work in {{ill|Yunnan|en|Yunnan|WD=Q43194|lt=Yunnan Province}}, China. Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]

'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of {{ill|Clay|en|Clay|WD=Q42302|lt=clay}}, {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quartz sand|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石英|1=quartz|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43010}}, {{ill|Limestone|en|Limestone|WD=Q23757|lt=limestone}}, and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/non-metallic materials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 非金属元素|1=nonmetal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19600}} to produce {{ill|Pottery|en|Pottery|WD=Q11642|lt=ceramics}}, {{ill|Roof tiles|en|Roof tiles|WD=Q268547|lt=tiles}}, {{ill|Glass|en|Glass|WD=Q11469|lt=glass}}, {{ill|Cement|en|Cement|WD=Q45190|lt=cement}}, and other {{ill|Ceramic|en|Ceramic|WD=Q45621|lt=ceramics}} (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of {{ill|Kiln|en|Kiln|WD=Q1724341|lt=kilns}}. Traditional fuels for kilns included {{ill|Firewood|en|Firewood|WD=Q35808|lt=firewood}}, {{ill|Char (chemistry)|en|Char (chemistry)|WD=Q11567121|lt=charcoal}}, and {{ill|Coal|en|Coal|WD=Q24489|lt=coal}}, but modern kilns often use {{ill|Petroleum|en|Petroleum|WD=Q22656|lt=oil-fired boilers}} or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/electric kilns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯|1=kiln|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1724341}}.

In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/thatch|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茅葺|1=thatched roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q874531}} or {{ill|Thatching|en|Thatching|WD=Q874531|lt=straw roofs}}, but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce {{ill|Porcelain|en|Porcelain|WD=Q130693|lt=porcelain}} and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal glass|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クリスタル・ガラス|1=lead glass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392551}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/potters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陶芸家|1=ceramicist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7541856}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic artists|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ceramicist|WD=Q7541856}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, {{ill|Cloisonné|en|Cloisonné|WD=Q1102226|lt=cloisonné}} and {{ill|Glass|en|Glass|WD=Q11469|lt=glass art}} were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many {{ill|Developing country|en|Developing country|WD=Q177323|lt=developing countries}} with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like {{ill|Roof tiles|en|Roof tiles|WD=Q268547|lt=tiles}}, alongside ceramics and tableware.

In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siding materials|1=siding materials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597728}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of {{ill|Carpentry|en|Carpentry|WD=Q203605|lt=carpenters}} and {{ill|Plasterwork|en|Plasterwork|WD=Q572879|lt=plasterers}} have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.

== Education ==

* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct.
* '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|1=Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11384748}} remains the sole institution of its kind.

Producing {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} by splitting cylindrical ceramic forms fired in kilns.]]

'''Kilnwork''' (窯業, ''yōgyō'') refers to the industrial process of using high-temperature treatment of {{ill|Clay|en|Clay|WD=Q42302|lt=clay|1=Clay}}, {{ill|quartz sand|ja|ケイ砂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quartz sand|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石英|1=Quartz|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43010}}, {{ill|Limestone|en|Limestone|WD=Q23757|lt=limestone|1=Limestone}}, and other {{ill|non-metallic materials|ja|非金属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/non-metallic materials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 非金属元素|1=Nonmetal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19600}} to produce {{ill|Pottery|en|Pottery|WD=Q11642|lt=ceramics|1=Pottery}}, {{ill|Roof tiles|en|Roof tiles|WD=Q268547|lt=tiles|1=Roof tiles}}, {{ill|Glass|en|Glass|WD=Q11469|lt=glass|1=Glass}}, {{ill|Cement|en|Cement|WD=Q45190|lt=cement|1=Cement}}, and other {{ill|Ceramic|en|Ceramic|WD=Q45621|lt=ceramics|1=Ceramic}} (also referred to as ceramic products or kilnwork products)<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%AA%AF%E6%A5%AD-145615 "Sekai Daijiten 2nd Edition, Daijirin 3rd Edition"]</ref>. The term originates from the use of {{ill|Kiln|en|Kiln|WD=Q1724341|lt=kilns|1=Kiln}}. Traditional fuels for kilns included {{ill|Firewood|en|Firewood|WD=Q35808|lt=firewood|1=Firewood}}, {{ill|Char (chemistry)|en|Char (chemistry)|WD=Q11567121|lt=charcoal|1=Char (chemistry)}}, and {{ill|Coal|en|Coal|WD=Q24489|lt=coal|1=Coal}}, but modern kilns often use {{ill|Petroleum|en|Petroleum|WD=Q22656|lt=oil-fired boilers|1=Petroleum}} or {{ill|electric kilns|ja|電気窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/electric kilns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 窯|1=Kiln|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1724341}}.

In ancient times, most houses had {{ill|thatch|ja|草葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/thatch|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 茅葺|1=Thatching|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q874531}} or {{ill|Thatching|en|Thatching|WD=Q874531|lt=straw roofs|1=Thatching}}, but from the medieval period, {{ill|clay tiles|ja|粘土瓦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay tiles|1=clay roof tile|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604529}} began to be used for roofing, especially in houses of aristocrats, samurai, and monks. The ability to produce {{ill|Porcelain|en|Porcelain|WD=Q130693|lt=porcelain|1=Porcelain}} and {{ill|crystal glass|ja|クリスタルガラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal glass|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クリスタル・ガラス|1=Lead glass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392551}} elevated these items as valued furnishings, even gaining recognition as art objects. The professions of {{ill|potters|ja|陶工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/potters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陶芸家|1=Ceramist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7541856}} and {{ill|ceramic artists|ja|陶芸家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic artists|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ceramist|WD=Q7541856}} thus established themselves with considerable social status. Similarly, {{ill|Cloisonné|en|Cloisonné|WD=Q1102226|lt=cloisonné|1=Cloisonné}} and {{ill|Glass|en|Glass|WD=Q11469|lt=glass art|1=Glass}} were appreciated as decorative crafts. However, in many {{ill|Developing country|en|Developing country|WD=Q177323|lt=developing countries|1=Developing country}} with small-scale traditional kilnwork, a significant volume of production remains devoted to building materials like {{ill|Roof tiles|en|Roof tiles|WD=Q268547|lt=tiles|1=Roof tiles}}, alongside ceramics and tableware.

In recent years, {{ill|siding materials|ja|窯業系サイディング材|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siding materials|1=Q11597728|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597728}} categorized under kilnwork have become increasingly popular in construction. Their short construction time, excellent design properties, and ability to address labor shortages of {{ill|Carpentry|en|Carpentry|WD=Q203605|lt=carpenters|1=Carpentry}} and {{ill|Plasterwork|en|Plasterwork|WD=Q572879|lt=plasterers|1=Plasterwork}} have driven the market demand, capturing approximately 70% of the external wall share in detached housing.

== Education ==

* '''Kilnwork High Schools''': As of April 2021, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸窯業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Kilnwork High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}} was renamed {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|ja|愛知県立瀬戸工科高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Seto Technical High School|1=Seto High School of Technology and Engineering|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494276}}, making "kilnwork" as part of school names extinct.
* '''Specialized Kilnwork Schools''': The {{ill|Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|ja|佐賀県立有田窯業大学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|1=Saga Prefectural Arita Kilnwork College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11384748}} remains the sole institution of its kind.
